title: Vector Calculus Gradient Identities Decision Flow
description: A flowchart to help decide which gradient identity to apply when simplifying or computing the gradient of a function.
elements: Product of Scalar and Vector Function (fF)
relationships: Start with the expression you need to take the gradient of. Follow the paths: If it's a simple scalar function (f), use the definition. If it's a product of two scalars (fg), use the product rule. If it's a scalar multiplied by a vector (fF), use the scalar-vector product rule. If it's a dot product (F • G) or cross product (F × G), use the respective identity for vector fields.
